title: Milestone 1: Project & Git
subtitle: Build your first real Java app and push it to GitHub like a Pro.
goal: Combine math, and input to build a calculator, then save it on GitHub.

blocks.heading: Welcome to the Pro leagues
blocks.html:
You've learned enough to build real things. Pros don't just learn syntax; they build projects and share them. Today, we step outside the browser.

You will build a Tip Calculator on your own computer and push it to GitHub.

blocks.heading: Step 1: Install Java (JDK)
blocks.html: Go to Adoptium and install the latest Java Development Kit (JDK 21+). Make sure to check 'Set JAVA_HOME'.

blocks.heading: Step 2: Get an IDE
blocks.html: Download IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition. It is the absolute standard for Java pros.

blocks.heading: Step 3: Build the Tip Calculator
blocks.html: Create a file named Calculator.java. Write a program that asks for the bill amount, the tip percentage, and how many people are splitting it. Then, print out how much each person should pay.

blocks.caption: A hint for your Calculator.java

blocks.heading: Step 4: Push to GitHub
blocks.html:
Pros use Git to save their code. Create a GitHub account. Open the terminal inside IntelliJ and type:

git init
git add Calculator.java
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin <your-repo-url>
git push -u origin main

blocks.question: Why do programmers use GitHub?
options: To save code, track changes, and share with others
To make their computer run faster
blocks.explain: GitHub is like Google Drive for code. It saves your work and lets you show it off.

challenge.title: Complete your GitHub push
challenge.instructions: There is no in-browser code challenge for this milestone! Your challenge is to build the Tip Calculator on your PC and push it to GitHub. Once it's live on GitHub, you've completed this milestone.
challenge.starter: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) {} }
challenge.solution: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) {} }
challenge.passMsg: 🏆 Incredible! You are now a real Java developer with code on GitHub.
